name: Cerro de Somé and La Tranquera Reservoir Loop
name_original: Cerro de Somé y embalse de la Tranquera — desde Nuévalos
description: Challenging 8.1-mile jogging route from Nuévalos to Somé Peak, offering excellent views of La Tranquera Reservoir.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Comunidad de Calatayud traverse a varied landscape, characterized by river valleys, mountainous terrain, and unique gypsum steppes. The region features the Jalón River, which creates greenways and small valleys, alongside the dramatic canyons of the Mesa River. Pine and oak forests are found in areas like Sierra Vicor, while the Sierra de Armantes presents distinctive red and white clay hills. This diverse geography provides a range of running environments from gentle riverside paths to more challenging mountain trails. Best jogging routes around Comunidad de Calatayud The most popular jogging route is Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular trail running from Jaraba Sanctuary, a 10.0 miles (16.0 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route offers views of rock paintings and a waterfall. Another top favourite among local runners is Cerro de Somé and La Tranquera Reservoir Loop, a difficult 8.1 miles (13.1 km) path. This route features reservoir views and varied terrain around Cerro de Somé. Local runners also love the Estanque loop from Calatayud, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail leading through urban green spaces, often completed in about 31 minutes. Jogging around Comunidad de Calatayud is defined by riverine paths, mountainous areas, and unique gypsum steppes. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from easy urban loops to difficult mountain trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 470 runners have used komoot to explore Comunidad de Calatayud's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Comunidad de Calatayud?
A: There are over 55 running routes documented on komoot for Comunidad de Calatayud, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Comunidad de Calatayud?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for running. You'll find pleasant riverine paths along the Jalón River, greenways through orchards, and urban parks. For more challenging runs, there are mountainous areas like the Sierra de Armantes with its distinctive red and white clay hills, and the pine and oak forests of Sierra Vicor. Unique gypsum steppes also provide a different, lunar-like running experience.

Q: Are there options for different running abilities in Comunidad de Calatayud?
A: Yes, the region caters to all abilities. You can find easy urban loops, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31485127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estanque loop from Calatayud&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.0 miles (4.8 km) long. There are also 37 moderate routes and 16 difficult trails, including longer distances and more significant elevation changes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983229155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular trail running from Jaraba Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running routes with waterfalls in Comunidad de Calatayud?
A: Absolutely! The region is home to several beautiful waterfalls. You can experience them on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983229155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular trail running from Jaraba Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable waterfalls in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caprichosa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5119827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorreaderos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the jogging paths?
A: Yes, many running routes offer stunning views. The Mesa River has carved dramatic canyons with vertical walls, and the Mirador de los Buitres (Vultures' Viewpoint) in Calmarza provides exceptional vistas. You can also find routes with reservoir views, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983232628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Somé and La Tranquera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I discover while running in the region?
A: Beyond waterfalls and viewpoints, you can explore unique natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Far West Bilbilitano Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the extensive network of trails within the Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, known for its lush vegetation and diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, the Comunidad de Calatayud has a rich history. You can find routes that offer views of historical sites and castles. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Ayud loop from Coso de Margarita&lt;/a&gt; passes near a castle. Other historical highlights in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maluenda Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and Church of San Millán de Berdejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly running routes in Comunidad de Calatayud?
A: For families, the urban green spaces and riverside paths are excellent choices. The city of Calatayud has parks like Parque de la Serna, which features circuits suitable for runners. Many local trails (SL) are circular and less than 15 kilometers, with several under 5 kilometers, making them ideal for a family run or walk.

Q: Are the running trails in Comunidad de Calatayud dog-friendly?
A: While many outdoor areas in Comunidad de Calatayud are generally welcoming, specific regulations for dogs can vary by trail or natural park. It's always best to check the individual route details on komoot or local signage for any restrictions, especially regarding leash requirements or protected areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Comunidad de Calatayud?
A: The region offers good running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot, especially in open areas, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to run year-round, though higher elevations might experience colder conditions.

Q: Are there circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Comunidad de Calatayud are circular, which is convenient for starting and ending at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983229155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular trail running from Jaraba Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983232628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Somé and La Tranquera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Local Trails (SL) are also typically circular and often shorter, perfect for a quick loop.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Comunidad de Calatayud?
A: Runners frequently praise the variety of landscapes, from tranquil riverside greenways to challenging mountain trails and unique gypsum steppes. The well-structured network of trails and the opportunity to discover natural attractions like waterfalls and rock formations are also highly valued by the komoot community.
tips: The La Tranquera Reservoir in Nuévalos is a spectacular place to enjoy nature and panoramic views. Inaugurated in 1959, the reservoir was designed to regulate the flow of the Piedra River and ensure the water supply in the region.

Located at an altitude of 817 meters, we find beautiful views of the Tranquera reservoir from Cerro de Somé. The path to here is not marked, but it is clearly visible.
Not long ago, this area was burned but it is already growing and returning to normal.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as a "hard run" and is best suited for individuals with very good fitness due to its challenging elevation gain over 13.1 kilometers.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: On average, completing this jogging route takes approximately 1 hour and 37 minutes. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Somé Peak and La Tranquera Reservoir jogging route?
faqs.answer: You can expect to navigate mostly paved surfaces along this route, offering a consistent footing for jogging.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers incredibly picturesque scenery, especially around the La Tranquera Reservoir. Key highlights include the View of the Tranquera Reservoir and the stunning View of the Tranquera Reservoir from Cerro de Somé. You'll enjoy beautiful views of the lake's crystal clear waters and surrounding pine trees.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Nuévalos. While specific parking details aren't provided, Nuévalos is the designated starting point, and you should be able to find parking within the town.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally considered dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, and be mindful of other trail users and any local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful scenery year-round. For the most comfortable jogging experience, consider visiting during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring cooler conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and jog this trail around Somé Peak and La Tranquera Reservoir.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Ibdes-Nuevalos, Senda a Somet, SL-Z 50, and Carretera Antigua trails.

faqs.question: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest near the trail?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural beauty of the reservoir, the starting point of Nuévalos itself has historical significance. You can explore local attractions such as a 16th-century Bishop's House and the fortress church of San Julián and Santa Bárbara.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, we recommend bringing appropriate jogging attire, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection, and a fully charged phone for navigation and emergencies.

waypoints.name: View of the Tranquera Reservoir from Cerro de Somé
